title: Termination of Employment
text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement by providing {{notice_period_days}} days' prior written notice. The Employer may terminate this Agreement immediately for cause including misconduct, gross negligence, fraud, or wilful violation of the Employer's policies, subject to compliance with the principles of natural justice including issuance of a show-cause notice and opportunity to respond. Upon termination, the Employee shall return all Company property and Confidential Information. The Employee shall be entitled to all accrued salary, earned leave encashment, and statutory dues up to the date of termination.
statutory_reference: Industrial Disputes Act, 1947 – S.25F; Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972
name: Termination of Employment

legal_basis.note: Procedure for retrenchment — last in first out
Right to livelihood — natural justice in termination
Time for payment of wages on termination

source: Industrial Disputes Act, 1947; Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972

jurisdiction_constraints.general: IDA 1947 Chapter VB applies to establishments with 100+ workers — prior government permission for retrenchment
jurisdiction_constraints.Maharashtra: Industrial Employment Standing Orders certified orders govern termination procedure

dispute_hotspots: Wrongful termination — labour court reinstates with back wages
Absence of natural justice — charge sheet + domestic enquiry mandatory before dismissal of workman
Retrenchment compensation — S.25F compensation mandatory even for 'poor performers'
Gratuity forfeiture — only permissible for specific misconduct under PGA S.4(6)
